title: Kanaka Durga Temple
tagline: Kanaka Durga Temple — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Kanaka Durga Temple, officially known as Sri Durga Malleswara Swamyvarla Devasthanam, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Kanaka Durga.
overview.description: Kanaka Durga Temple, officially known as Sri Durga Malleswara Swamyvarla Devasthanam, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Kanaka Durga. The deity in this temple is also popularly referred as Kanaka Durga. The temple is located in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, India on the Indrakeeladri hill on the banks of Krishna River. Kaalika Purana, Durgaa Sapthashati and other Vedic literature have mentioned about Kanaka Durga on the Indrakeelaadri and have described the deity as Swayambhu, (self-manifested) in Triteeya Kalpa.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Akkanna Madanna Caves
topPlaces.description: Akkanna Madanna caves are a group of rock-cut Hindu cave temples in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, India. Dated to mid 7th-century and dedicated to Shiva, they were excavated by the Eastern Chalukya dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Kanakadurga Flyover
topPlaces.description: The Kanakadurga Flyover (also known as Durga Temple Flyover) is a flyover spanning the Krishna River and Prakasam Barrage in Vijayawada, India. As of 2022, it is the longest flyover in Andhra Pradesh with a length of 2.6 kilometres (1.6 mi).

topPlaces.name: Prakasam Barrage
topPlaces.description: The Prakasam Barrage is a 1,223.5-metre-long (4,014 ft) tidal barrage across the Krishna River connecting Vijayawada and Guntur districts in Andhra Pradesh, India. The barrage serves also as a road bridge that spans a lake, formed by the barrage wall.

topPlaces.name: Pandit Nehru Bus Station
topPlaces.description: The Pandit Nehru Bus Station (PNBS), also known as the Telugu Satavahana Prayana Pranganam, is a bus station in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, India situated on the southern side of the city, adjacent to the Krishna River. It is owned by the Andhra Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation (APSRTC) and serves as a key transportation hub for the region.

topPlaces.name: Gandhi Hill, Vijayawada
topPlaces.description: The Gandhi Hill (elevation 500 ft (150 m)) is a hill in Vijayawada, situated behind the Vijayawada railway station in the Tarapet area. A Gandhi Memorial, built on this hill, is the first in the country to have seven stupas.

topPlaces.name: Vijayawada
topPlaces.description: Vijayawada ( Vijay-uh-waw-duh), also called as Bezawada, is the second largest city and a major commercial hub in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. The city forms an integral part of the Andhra Pradesh Capital Region and is situated on the banks of the Krishna River, flanked by the Eastern Ghats and the Indrakeeladri Hills.

topPlaces.name: Electric Loco Shed, Vijayawada
topPlaces.description: Electric Loco Shed, Vijayawada is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Vijayawada of the South Coast Railway zone in Andhra Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: 2020 Vijayawada fire
topPlaces.description: On 9 August 2020 at around 5 am IST, a major fire broke out at a COVID-19 facility located at the Hotel Swarna Palace in the city of Vijayawada, in the state of Andhra Pradesh. The fire killed 11 people and wounded a further 22.

items.note: Akkanna Madanna caves are a group of rock-cut Hindu cave temples in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, India. Dated to mid 7th-century and dedicated to Shiva, they were excavated by the Eastern Chalukya dynasty.

The Kanakadurga Flyover (also known as Durga Temple Flyover) is a flyover spanning the Krishna River and Prakasam Barrage in Vijayawada, India. As of 2022, it is the longest flyover in Andhra Pradesh with a length of 2.6 kilometres (1.6 mi).

The Prakasam Barrage is a 1,223.5-metre-long (4,014 ft) tidal barrage across the Krishna River connecting Vijayawada and Guntur districts in Andhra Pradesh, India. The barrage serves also as a road bridge that spans a lake, formed by the barrage wall.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Pandit Nehru Bus Station & around

items.note: The Pandit Nehru Bus Station (PNBS), also known as the Telugu Satavahana Prayana Pranganam, is a bus station in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, India situated on the southern side of the city, adjacent to the Krishna River. It is owned by the Andhra Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation (APSRTC) and serves as a key transportation hub for the region.

The Gandhi Hill (elevation 500 ft (150 m)) is a hill in Vijayawada, situated behind the Vijayawada railway station in the Tarapet area. A Gandhi Memorial, built on this hill, is the first in the country to have seven stupas.

Vijayawada ( Vijay-uh-waw-duh), also called as Bezawada, is the second largest city and a major commercial hub in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. The city forms an integral part of the Andhra Pradesh Capital Region and is situated on the banks of the Krishna River, flanked by the Eastern Ghats and the Indrakeeladri Hills.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Electric Loco Shed, Vijayawada & around

items.note: Electric Loco Shed, Vijayawada is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Vijayawada of the South Coast Railway zone in Andhra Pradesh, India.

On 9 August 2020 at around 5 am IST, a major fire broke out at a COVID-19 facility located at the Hotel Swarna Palace in the city of Vijayawada, in the state of Andhra Pradesh. The fire killed 11 people and wounded a further 22.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kanaka Durga Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kanaka Durga Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kanaka Durga Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Vijayawada Airport (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Vijayawada Junction (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kanaka Durga Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kanaka Durga Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kanaka Durga Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kanaka Durga Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kanaka Durga Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Akkanna Madanna Caves, Kanakadurga Flyover, Prakasam Barrage, Pandit Nehru Bus Station, Gandhi Hill, Vijayawada.
